reasoning: Q1 shows excellent optimization trajectory (62→48 ms, 22.6% improvement, both iterations positive). Current performance is elite: 48 ms vs DuckDB 84 ms (43% faster) and PostgreSQL 15,460 ms (320x faster). Remaining bottleneck is I/O-bound (2.3 GB sequential HDD reads). This is the final iteration (3/3); applying Zone Map Skipping (low risk, 10-20% I/O reduction) offers best ROI for closing the remaining performance gap.
focus_areas: Zone Map metadata loading and block-level filtering to skip blocks where min(l_shipdate) > CUTOFF_DATE
Reduce I/O volume from 2.3 GB to 1.8-2.0 GB via storage-level predicate pushdown
strategy_notes: Zone Map Skipping (Priority 1, low risk) targets the dominant bottleneck (HDD I/O latency). Expected 5-10 ms improvement (10-20% reduction). Implementation requires: (1) load zone_map metadata, (2) pre-filter morsel assignment, (3) verify no blocks are incorrectly skipped. This is the highest-impact, lowest-risk optimization available. Do not pursue SIMD or Kahan optimizations in this final iteration—they offer marginal gains (1-2 ms) with higher risk (medium-high). After this iteration, Q1 will reach its I/O efficiency ceiling on HDD; future improvements require hardware changes (faster storage) or fundamental query rewrites.
notes: GenDB Q1 is now competitive with elite OLAP systems. Focus remaining effort on maximizing impact within constraints: single, well-targeted optimization in the final iteration. If Zone Map Skipping achieves expected 10-20% gain, Q1 will reach ~38-40 ms (near-optimal for HDD-backed sequential scan). Subsequent optimization cycles should focus on Q3 and Q6.
